# Renewal Process

**A renewal process is a counting process generated by positive finite i.i.d. interoccurrence times.**

## Definition

Let $X_1,X_2,\ldots$ be positive finite i.i.d. random variables with distribution $F$. Define renewal epochs
$$
W_n=X_1+\cdots+X_n,\qquad W_0=0,
$$
and counting process
$$
N(t)=\max(n:W_n\leq t).
$$

## Key Properties

- $N(t)\geq k$ iff $W_k\leq t$.
- The renewal function is $M(t)=\mathbb{E}[N(t)]$.
- If $\mu=\mathbb{E}[X]$, then $N(t)/t\to1/\mu$ a.s.
- The Poisson process is the exponential-interarrival special case.

## Intuition

At each renewal epoch the system starts statistically fresh, so long-run behaviour can be read from one typical cycle.

## Connection to Other Concepts

- [[renewal-function]] gives expected counts.
- [[excess-life]] studies residual time to the next renewal.
- [[renewal-reward-process]] adds rewards per cycle.
- [[regenerative-process]] generalizes renewal instants to full process restarts.

- [[elementary-renewal-theorem]]

## Examples

- Light bulb replacement.
- Poisson process.
- Alternating busy/idle cycles.
